mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns as borrowing costs fall and liquidity expands.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 60% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity repricing.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in 70% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales growth and deceleration in housing starts indicates a shift in consumer demand toward services and away from durable goods, compressing corporate profit margins and driving negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a positive rank correlation with 60-day forward returns in 65% of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices during periods of high inflation indicate supply-side bottlenecks that force central banks to tighten policy, resulting in negative forward equity returns as growth expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in 60% of months when industrial production rises alongside CPI above 235.0.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level above 2.5 percent combined with rising real yields increases the cost of capital for growth-oriented equities, generating negative forward S&P 500 returns as discount rates compress valuations.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns exceed zero in 70% of observations when the federal funds rate surpasses 2.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates signals labor market tightness that accelerates wage inflation, prompting anticipatory equity sell-offs as profit margin compression becomes inevitable.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields a positive rank IC above 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows or fails to predict negative 60-day returns in 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth during late-cycle expansions exhausts productive capacity and triggers inflationary pressures, leading to negative forward equity returns as markets price in impending monetary tightening and growth deceleration.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in 65% of quarters where real GDP growth exceeds 3.0 percent during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in the industrial production index reflect expanding manufacturing output and corporate revenue growth, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or 5-day forward returns are negative in 60% of months with rising industrial production.
